title: ampacus roxburghiana
intro: Ampacus roxburghiana, also known as the Roxburgh's Ampacus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The Roxburgh's Ampacus is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving pain, as well as aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of the Roxburgh's Ampacus include infusions and decoctions, which can be used to make teas and tonics, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
